LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS AKAY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS AKAY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON ANY OR ALL OF THE FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN HIS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT MARTHE SCHREIBER CREATES TRIABLE DISPUTES WHETHER SOME OR ALL OF THE MONEY CLAIMED BY MR. AKAY IS OWED BY MS. SCHREIBER. THAT EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THERE ARE MATERIAL DISPUTES WHETHER: 1) MR. AKAY HAD THE AUTHORITY TO PERFORM SOME OF THE SERVICES HE PERFORMED FOR WHICH HE IS SEEKING DAMAGES; AND 2) WHETHER THERE WAS ANY VALUE AND, IF SO, THE EXTENT OF THE VALUE OF SOME OF THE SERVICES PERFORMED BY MR. AKAY FOR WHICH HE IS SEEKING DAMAGES.ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

DECLARATION OF WILLIAM S. WEISBERG IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS REPLY TO PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO HER MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100019780) FILED BY DEFENDANT SCHREIBER, MARTHE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARTHE SCHREIBER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT MARTHE SCHREIBER'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS AKAY ESTABLISHES THAT HE PERSONALLY SERVED MS. SCHREIBER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 415.10. MS. SCHREIBER'S RELIANCE ON THE MEDIATION CONFIDENTIALITY STATUTES LACKS MERIT. THE PURPOSE OF THE MEDIATION CONFIDENTIALITY STATUTES IS "TO ENCOURAGE THE USE OF MEDIATION BY PROMOTING A CANDID AND INFORMAL EXCHANGE REGARDING EVENTS IN THE PAST....THIS FRANK EXCHANGE IS ACHIEVED ONLY IF THE PARTICIPANTS KNOW THAT WHAT IS SAID IN THE MEDIATION WILL NOT BE USED TO THEIR DETRIMENT THROUGH LATER COURT PROCEEDINGS AND OTHER ADJUDICATORY PROCESSES." (CASSEL V. SUP. CRT. (2011) 51 CAL.4TH 113, 123 (INTERNAL QUOTATIONS OMITTED.) SERVICE OF PROCESS IN THIS CASE IS NOT A COMMUNICATION "FOR THE PURPOSE OF, IN THE COURSE OF, OR PURSUANT TO, A MEDIATION" WITHIN THE MEANING OF EVIDENCE CODE 1119(A). THE AMOUNT, IF ANY, OWED BY MS. SCHREIBER TO MR. AKAY WAS NOT INCLUDED IN THE MATTERS THAT WERE BEING MEDIATED AND THE SERVICE MADE ON MS. SCHREIBER OCCURRED PRIOR TO THE START OF THE MEDIATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice
Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Advanced From Nov-05-18 To Nov-01-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice
Off Calendar Nov-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marthe Schreiber, 415-309-5404 Defendant in Pro Per.
No Other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS AKAY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Plaintiff Douglas Akay's motion for summary judgment on any or all of the four causes of action alleged in his first amended complaint is denied in its entirety. The evidence submitted by defendant Marthe Schreiber creates triable disputes whether some or all of the money claimed by Mr. Akay is owed by Ms. Schreiber. That evidence shows that there are material disputes whether: 1) Mr. Akay had the authority to perform some of the services he performed for which he is seeking damages; and 2) whether there was any value and, if so, the extent of the value of some of the services performed by Mr. Akay for which he is seeking damages.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Atty. William S. Weisberg of Weisberg & Miller, Tel. No. 1-415-296-7070 Moving party;

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MARTHE SCHREIBER's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint as follows: Defendant Marthe Schreiber's motion to quash service of summons and complaint is denied. Plaintiff Douglas Akay establishes that he personally served Ms. Schreiber in accordance with CCP 415.10. Ms. Schreiber's reliance on the mediation confidentiality statutes lacks merit. The purpose of the mediation confidentiality statutes is "to encourage the use of mediation by promoting a candid and informal exchange regarding events in the past....This frank exchange is achieved only if the participants know that what is said in the mediation will not be used to their detriment through later court proceedings and other adjudicatory processes." (Cassel v. Sup. Crt. (2011) 51 Cal.4th 113, 123 (internal quotations omitted.) Service of process in this case is not a communication "for the purpose of, in the course of, or pursuant to, a mediation" within the meaning of Evidence Code 1119(a). The amount, if any, owed by Ms. Schreiber to Mr. Akay was not included in the matters that were being mediated and the service made on Ms. Schreiber occurred prior to the start of the mediation. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
